Name: Notre Dame Rocketry Team (NDRT)
Relevance: Designs and builds complex high-powered rockets with mission-oriented payloads for the NASA Student Launch Initiative.

Name: IrishSat
Relevance: Develops low earth orbit platforms by designing and operating CubeSats carrying novel research payloads.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) ND Chapter
Relevance: The university's only undergraduate aerospace professional society, overseeing major space and flight design teams.

Name: Design, Build, Fly (DBF)
Relevance: An AIAA-affiliated student team focusing on the hands-on design, construction, and flight of unmanned aircraft.

Name: Cosmos
Relevance: The official campus astronomy club that hosts stargazing events and operates the 32-inch Krizmanich research telescope.

Name: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Club (UAV ND)
Relevance: Focuses on autonomous flight, aerial robotics, and custom flight control software development for UAV applications.

Name: Hessert Laboratory for Aerospace Research
Relevance: Operates 19 high-speed wind tunnels to support innovations in hypersonic flight, aerodynamics, and space exploration components.

Name: Center for Astrophysics at Notre Dame University (CANDU)
Relevance: Conducts research on exoplanets, stellar evolution, supernovae, the Big Bang, and cosmology.

Name: Institute for Structure and Nuclear Astrophysics (ISNAP)
Relevance: Operates the Nuclear Science Laboratory focusing on nucleosynthesis and stellar element evolution in supernovae.

Name: Notre Dame Power & Propulsion (ND P&P)
Relevance: Leads major development and testing in high Mach air-breathing propulsion, gas-turbine engines, and hypersonic flight systems.

Name: Sigma Gamma Tau
Relevance: The national aerospace engineering honor society recognizing student excellence in aerospace-related fields.

Name: Robotics @ Notre Dame
Relevance: A collaborative group of roboticists focusing on autonomous systems; their work in intelligent navigation and control is highly applicable to space rovers and autonomous flight.

Name: Joint Institute for Nuclear Astrophysics (JINA-CEE)
Relevance: An NSF physics frontier center studying the evolution of elements; Notre Dame is a core founding institution, though the center itself spans multiple universities.
